prime_audit: S68 P1 PRIME-AUDIT (did not rubber-stamp the reviewer). GROK-ANALYST returned FIX-THEN-SHIP w/ 5 points; ALL 5 REFUTED empirically: (1) ReadonlyArray tightens contract + lost exhaustiveness -> FALSE: NetDiagnostics.strategies is ALREADY ReadonlyArray<StrategyDiagnostic> at the call site (transport.ts:88), param type MATCHES what td.strategies already was (no tightening); original inline had NO exhaustiveness check and .filter did not narrow there either -> char-identical, zero divergence. (2) indexed-access leaks StrategyDiagnostic into .d.ts, regresses S67 -> FALSE: project builds tsc -b --noEmit = NO .d.ts emitted (private app); the NAME stays unexported; structural shape already reachable via exported NetDiagnostics; knip 0 confirms no regression. (4) test fixtures cannot catch NetDiagnostics-vs-getDiagnostics drift -> FALSE: getDiagnostics():NetDiagnostics by declaration so td.strategies IS exactly NetDiagnostics[strategies] -- no drift surface; tsc PASS proves call-site assignability. (5) unicode foot-gun; smoke not in CI -> FALSE: new vitest cases assert the exact glyphs + vitest RUNS in CI -> a future encoding regression WOULD fail CI (net improvement over the untested inline original). NONE identified a real behavior change / build break / missed consumer / knip regression. PDR mandated VERBATIM extraction; adding exhaustiveness/DEV-guards would DEVIATE -> correctly declined. Runtime-Verifiability satisfied via in-browser module execution (not just static parse). Mirrors S66/S67 (Grok FUD -> empirical refute). VERDICT: SHIP.

reflexion_entries_to_archive: P1 #strategysummary-extract #indexed-access-avoids-reexport #self-driven-boot-smoke-unblocks-attended #grok-fud-refuted-x5: extracted the byte-identical lobby wire-diagnostic strategy-summary builder (2x in main.ts: joiner ~810-819 + host ~839-848) into pure src/net/strategySummary.ts formatStrategySummary() + 8-case unit test; both sites -> formatStrategySummary(td.strategies); main.ts -17 LOC (948->931). KEY DESIGN: typed the param as indexed-access NetDiagnostics[strategies] instead of importing StrategyDiagnostic -- the naive approach would RE-EXPORT a type S67 just deliberately unexported (knip regression + reversal of prior work); indexed-access keeps StrategyDiagnostic internal, zero transport.ts edits, knip stays 0. The test uses the same trick: type Strategy = NetDiagnostics[strategies][number] for typed fixtures without the non-exported name. UNBLOCKED THE S67 COUNCIL DEFER: S67 deferred this as attended because boot-smoke needed live eyes; I self-drove it via preview MCP -- dynamically imported the REAL Vite-transformed module in the running dev server and executed it (byte-identical incl U+2026/U+2713 glyphs), which is STRONGER than eyeballing pixels (proves the shipped artifact runs in the browser runtime, not just node/vitest) AND proportionate (the live lobby strip needs real multi-peer, a separate netcode item, and the render path is unchanged). GROK-ANALYST FIX-THEN-SHIP w/ 5 points -> ALL 5 REFUTED on PRIME-AUDIT (ReadonlyArray already the call-site type; noEmit=no .d.ts; dev-only doc already present; getDiagnostics():NetDiagnostics so no drift; glyph asserts now IN CI vitest). LESSON: when extracting a fn whose param type is a non-exported interface, type via INDEXED-ACCESS on the exported container (NetDiagnostics[strategies][number]) -- preserves encapsulation, no re-export, no knip regression.
